mnemonics: SHARE: Simultaneous access, Hierarchical directories, Access rights, Remote file systems, Extensions
keyPoints: File sharing allows multiple users to access the same file concurrently.
Access control mechanisms (like permissions and ACLs) are crucial for security and integrity.
Different sharing semantics (e.g., UNIX, session, immutable) define how updates are visible to processes.
commonMistakes: Assuming all operating systems use the same file locking mechanisms (e.g., advisory vs. mandatory).
Overlooking the security implications of setting overly permissive access rights on shared files.
